issues.title: Jimmy Clay & Roy Kizer Golf Course Rezoning Initiation
issues.summary: Resolution initiating rezoning of approximately 445 acres of City-owned land (Jimmy Clay and Roy Kizer golf courses) at 5400 Jimmy Clay Drive. Sponsored by CMs Fuentes, Alter, Ellis, and Laine. This could be one of the largest single housing development opportunities in Austin if the land is eventually rezoned for residential/mixed-use.

public_comment.instructions: This was an initiation resolution — the actual rezoning process will have extensive public hearings. Monitor for future hearing dates.

issues.why_it_matters_to_you: 445 acres of potential housing on city-owned land is a massive pro-development opportunity. Aligns directly with your YIMBY values. The initiation vote signals council intent; the actual rezoning fights will come later and will need organized pro-housing voices.

issues.title: Convention Center Redevelopment — Petition Challenge
issues.summary: Austin United PAC is backing a petition to halt the $1.6 billion Austin Convention Center redevelopment and redirect hotel tax revenue to arts and cultural uses. The PAC raised $33K+ including $7K from Save Our Springs Alliance. If the petition qualifies, it could go to voters.

public_comment.instructions: Monitor petition signature gathering progress. If it qualifies for ballot, this becomes a major election issue.

issues.why_it_matters_to_you: As a pro-development pragmatist, you'd likely oppose this petition — convention center investment drives economic development. But the anti-development coalition behind it (SOS Alliance) overlaps with NIMBY interests you're already monitoring. Worth tracking.

issues.title: City Council District 1 — Open Seat Election
issues.summary: CM Natasha Harper-Madison is term-limited. District 1 (East Austin, including 78702) will have an open seat for the first time in 8 years. Current declared candidates: Steven Brown (positions himself as anti-developer, constituent accountability focused) and Alexandria Anderson. Filing deadline Aug 17 — more candidates expected.

public_comment.instructions: Monitor candidate announcements. Evaluate housing/development positions carefully. Consider early engagement or donation to most-aligned candidate.

issues.why_it_matters_to_you: This is YOUR district's council seat and it's open for the first time in 8 years. The winner will represent 78702 for the next 4 years on every housing and development vote. Steven Brown's stated positions (anti-developer framing, 'fighting for residents over developers') may conflict with your YIMBY priorities. This is the single most important local race for you in 2026.

issues.title: Commissioner Precinct 4 — Democratic Runoff
issues.summary: Democratic primary runoff for Travis County Commissioner, Precinct 4. Susanna Ledesma Morales led in the March primary. This position controls county-level decisions on land use, transportation infrastructure, and budget allocation for eastern Travis County.

public_comment.instructions: Verify voter registration at votetexas.gov. Research both candidates' positions on development and infrastructure.

issues.why_it_matters_to_you: Runoff elections have extremely low turnout — often 8-12% of registered voters. Your individual vote has outsized impact. The commissioner directly influences county-level land use and transportation decisions that affect your daily life. This is the highest impact-per-effort political action available to you in the next 90 days.

issues.title: Springdale Farm Zoning Follow-Up — District 3
issues.summary: Follow-up staff report on the Springdale Farm zoning case (C14-2026-0004) in District 3, near East Austin. A neighborhood postponement request has been filed. This is an example of the neighborhood vs. development tension playing out at the parcel level.

public_comment.instructions: Planning Commission meetings allow public comment. Check austintexas.gov for sign-up details.

issues.why_it_matters_to_you: Adjacent to your area. The neighborhood postponement tactic is a classic NIMBY delay strategy worth monitoring. Each individual case sets precedent for how similar cases in your neighborhood will be handled.

issues.title: Austin Housing Finance Corp — $5.5M Loan Authorization
issues.summary: Council authorized a $5.5 million AHFC loan for affordable housing. The council recesses its regular meeting to convene as the AHFC Board of Directors for these items.
issues.why_it_matters_to_you: Aligned with your housing values. Low contestedness means your engagement isn't needed here, but worth tracking as part of the city's overall housing investment picture.

issues.title: Raising Travis County — $5M Childcare Investment
issues.summary: Transfer of $5,046,004 from Raising Travis County fund to support interlocal agreement with City of Austin for childcare quality improvement and out-of-school time capacity, plus $80K for communications consulting.
issues.why_it_matters_to_you: Lower on your personal salience list but represents good governance — evidence-based investment in childcare infrastructure. The kind of pragmatic spending you'd support.
